name: Proton charge radius puzzle
summary: The 2010 measurement of the proton charge radius via muonic-hydrogen Lamb-shift spectroscopy (Pohl et al., r_p = 0.84184(67) fm) disagreed by ~5 sigma with the CODATA value from electron-hydrogen spectroscopy and electron-proton scattering (~0.8768(69) fm). The 4% discrepancy challenged either QED theory predictions, hadronic structure modelling, or pointed at lepton-non-universal new physics. Subsequent precision e-H measurements (Beyer 2017, Bezginov 2019, Fleurbaey 2018) have largely converged on the muonic-hydrogen value, formally resolving the tension while leaving residual interpretive questions.

characteristic_numbers.note: Pohl et al. 2010, Nature 466, 213. 5 sigma below electron-based determinations.
Modern e-H spectroscopy (2017-2022) has shifted toward the muonic value; CODATA 2022 quotes r_p = 0.8414 fm.

principles_stressed.note: Original 5 sigma discrepancy could have been interpreted as new lepton-non-universal physics (a few-MeV scalar coupling to muons but not electrons). Resolution with newer e-H measurements largely closes that channel; remaining work is to fully understand systematics.

history: Pohl et al. (2010), 'The size of the proton', Nature 466, 213 — 5 sigma discrepancy. Antognini et al. (2013, Science 339, 417) refined the muonic-H measurement. Modern e-H measurements: Beyer et al. (2017, Science 358, 79), Bezginov et al. (2019, Science 365, 1007), Fleurbaey et al. (2018, PRL 120, 183001). 2022 CODATA largely converges on the muonic-H value.

references.citation: Pohl, Antognini, Nez et al., 'The size of the proton', Nature 466, 213 (2010)
Antognini et al., 'Proton structure from the measurement of 2S-2P transition frequencies of muonic hydrogen', Science 339, 417 (2013)
